body:
ac-12 partial: pre-release brownfield dry-run against finetype exposed two scope-gaps that aren't this spec's to fix.

(1) The Transform handler for `ac_type` values is correct end-to-end — proven by 11 unit tests covering canonical pass-through, typo normalisation, build/eval keyword routing, and the two quarantine fallbacks.

(2) The dry-run against finetype produced 0 dispositions in the run summary because 54 spec.yaml files fail typed re-parse for OTHER brownfield drift: missing top-level `id`, scalar AC entries (just strings, not structured mappings), pre-orbit-state schema shape. The reconcile reporter only surfaces dispositions after `value_to_canonical::<T>` succeeds — Transform's mutations on these files are correct in memory but invisible in the report. arcform additionally uses `orbit/` rather than `.orbit/` so the binary is blind to its substrate.

(3) Closing this gap requires reconcile-mode v3 rules: filename-derived id, scalar-AC wrapping, possibly a layout discrimination for `orbit/` vs `.orbit/`. That's a separate card / spec — see memo 2026-05-16-richer-reconcile-rules.

ac-12 is force-closed at spec.close with this note as the audit record.

force-close: ac-12 unfinished — pre-release brownfield dry-run uncovered out-of-scope reconcile drift requiring follow-up (see .orbit/memos/2026-05-16-richer-reconcile-rules.md). Transform handler correctness proven via 11 unit tests; end-to-end brownfield validation deferred. ac-14 (post-release brew smoke on beelink) defers cleanly via deferrable_open as ac_type: observation.
